title: Integrated Pest Management Decision Flowchart
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive vis-network decision tree guiding students through the IPM hierarchy of pest control strategies across three crop scenarios

learningObjectives: Apply the IPM decision-making hierarchy to determine appropriate pest control strategies
Evaluate the ecological trade-offs of cultural, biological, and chemical pest controls
Compare IPM approaches across different pest scenarios and crop systems
prerequisites: Familiarity with types of pests and their crop impacts

keyQuestions: Why does IPM prioritize cultural and biological controls before chemical ones?
How do the ecological impacts differ between the three pest scenarios?
What long-term consequences arise from skipping directly to chemical controls?
How does the economic threshold concept influence IPM decisions?
commonMisconceptions: IPM means never using pesticides -- it actually means using them as a last resort in a systematic hierarchy
All pest control methods have similar ecological impacts -- in reality, chemical controls typically have much higher ecological costs
IPM is less effective than conventional pest control -- studies show IPM can be equally effective with lower environmental impact
assessmentOpportunities: Have students design an IPM plan for a novel pest scenario not covered in the simulation
Compare student decision paths and discuss why different groups may choose different strategies
Ask students to calculate the cumulative ecological cost of different decision paths
